   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-01",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Objects, nearest nodes, and graph projection\n",
    "\n",
    "This notebook shows how to match external objects to a graph and how to project them onto nearest graph edges.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-02",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-07T12:10:45.707128Z",

   },
   "source": [
    "# To install IduEdu in a clean environment:\n",
    "# !pip install IduEdu\n",

    "OSM_ID = 1114252\n"
   ],

   "source": [
    "import geopandas as gpd\n",
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "import pandas as pd\n",
    "from shapely.geometry import LineString, Point\n",
    "\n",
    "from iduedu import get_drive_graph\n",
    "\n",
    "G = get_drive_graph(osm_id=OSM_ID, simplify=True, keep_largest_subgraph=True)\n",
    "G\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stderr",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "2026-07-07 15:18:36.038 | WARNING  | Removing 38 nodes outside the largest strongly connected component. Retaining 707 of 745 nodes.\n"
     ]
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "text/plain": [
       "UrbanGraph(nodes=707, edges=1188, is_multigraph=True, is_directed=True, edge_direction_column='oneway', crs='EPSG:32636', type='drive')"
      ]
     },
     "execution_count": 2,
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "execute_result"
    }
   ],
   "execution_count": 2
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-04",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Create simulated objects near roads\n",
    "\n",
    "The example below creates a small synthetic object layer. We take several road segments, place points on them, and then shift those points perpendicular to the road. This imitates buildings or services located near, but not directly on, the road centerline.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-05",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.216343Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.215829Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.235872Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.235367Z"
    },
    "ExecuteTime": {
     "end_time": "2026-07-07T12:18:36.120117Z",
     "start_time": "2026-07-07T12:18:36.101543Z"
    }
   },
   "source": [
    "def line_perpendicular_offset_point(line, fraction=0.5, distance=18.0, side=1):\n",
    "    # Return a point shifted perpendicular to a line for visual test objects.\n",
    "    point = line.interpolate(fraction, normalized=True)\n",
    "    distance_along = line.project(point)\n",
    "    eps = max(min(line.length * 0.03, 8.0), 1.0)\n",
    "    before = line.interpolate(max(distance_along - eps, 0.0))\n",
    "    after = line.interpolate(min(distance_along + eps, line.length))\n",
    "\n",
    "    dx = after.x - before.x\n",
    "    dy = after.y - before.y\n",
    "    norm = float(np.hypot(dx, dy))\n",
    "    if norm == 0:\n",
    "        return point\n",
    "\n",
    "    return Point(\n",
    "        point.x + side * (-dy / norm) * distance,\n",
    "        point.y + side * (dx / norm) * distance,\n",
    "    )\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def make_focus_area(graph, radius=250):\n",
    "    # Choose a compact road-rich area near the graph center.\n",
    "    graph_center = graph.nodes_gdf.geometry.union_all().centroid\n",
    "    candidate_edges = graph.edges_gdf.loc[graph.edges_gdf.geometry.length > 45].copy()\n",
    "    seed_idx = candidate_edges.distance(graph_center).sort_values().index[0]\n",
    "    seed_point = candidate_edges.loc[seed_idx, \"geometry\"].interpolate(0.5, normalized=True)\n",
    "    return seed_point.buffer(radius)\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def make_simulated_objects(graph, focus_area, count=10, offset_m=50.0):\n",
    "    # Create simulated point objects near roads inside the focus area.\n",
    "    # The offset is intentionally visible so projection links are readable.\n",
    "    local_edges = graph.edges_gdf.loc[graph.edges_gdf.intersects(focus_area)].copy()\n",
    "    local_edges = local_edges.sort_values(\"length_meter\", ascending=False).head(count)\n",
    "\n",
    "    records = []\n",
    "    for i, (_, edge) in enumerate(local_edges.iterrows()):\n",
    "        fraction = 0.35 + 0.08 * (i % 4)\n",
    "        point = line_perpendicular_offset_point(\n",
    "            edge.geometry,\n",
    "            fraction=fraction,\n",
    "            distance=offset_m,\n",
    "        )\n",
    "        records.append({\"object_id\": i, \"name\": f\"simulated_object_{i}\", \"geometry\": point})\n",
    "\n",
    "    objects_gdf = gpd.GeoDataFrame(records, geometry=\"geometry\", crs=graph.crs).set_index(\"object_id\")\n",
    "    return objects_gdf\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "focus_area = make_focus_area(G)\n",
    "objects = make_simulated_objects(G, focus_area)\n",
    "objects\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {

   "source": [
    "def bounds_around_layers(*layers, margin=80):\n",
    "    # Return padded bounds around non-empty GeoDataFrames or GeoSeries.\n",
    "    bounds = []\n",
    "    for layer in layers:\n",
    "        if layer is None or len(layer) == 0:\n",
    "            continue\n",
    "        bounds.append(layer.total_bounds)\n",
    "    minx = min(b[0] for b in bounds) - margin\n",
    "    miny = min(b[1] for b in bounds) - margin\n",
    "    maxx = max(b[2] for b in bounds) + margin\n",
    "    maxy = max(b[3] for b in bounds) + margin\n",
    "    return minx, miny, maxx, maxy\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def roads_around_objects(graph, objects_gdf, padding=220):\n",
    "    # Select only roads and nodes near the example objects for readable plots.\n",
    "    plot_area = objects_gdf.geometry.union_all().convex_hull.buffer(padding)\n",
    "    roads = graph.edges_gdf.loc[graph.edges_gdf.intersects(plot_area)].copy()\n",
    "    nodes = graph.nodes_gdf.loc[graph.nodes_gdf.intersects(plot_area)].copy()\n",
    "    return plot_area, roads, nodes\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "def set_plot_window(ax, *layers, margin=80):\n",
    "    minx, miny, maxx, maxy = bounds_around_layers(*layers, margin=margin)\n",
    "    ax.set_xlim(minx, maxx)\n",
    "    ax.set_ylim(miny, maxy)\n",
    "    ax.set_aspect(\"equal\", adjustable=\"box\")\n",
    "    ax.set_axis_off()\n",
    "\n",
    "\n",
    "plot_area, local_roads, local_nodes = roads_around_objects(G, objects)\n",
    "print(\"local roads:\", len(local_roads))\n",
    "print(\"local nodes:\", len(local_nodes))\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "local roads: 29\n",
      "local nodes: 14\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "execution_count": 4
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-07",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Nearest graph nodes\n",
    "\n",
    "`nearest_nodes` maps each object geometry to the nearest existing graph node. This is fast and useful for coarse object-to-graph matching, but it does not change graph topology.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-08",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.247354Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.247354Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.255264Z",

    },
    "ExecuteTime": {
     "end_time": "2026-07-07T12:18:36.245139Z",
     "start_time": "2026-07-07T12:18:36.237139Z"
    }
   },
   "source": [
    "from iduedu import nearest_nodes\n",
    "\n",
    "objects[\"nearest_node\"] = nearest_nodes(G, objects)\n",
    "objects[[\"name\", \"nearest_node\"]]\n"
   ],

   "source": [
    "nearest_node_geoms = G.nodes_gdf.loc[objects[\"nearest_node\"], \"geometry\"]\n",
    "nearest_lines = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\n",
    "    geometry=[LineString([object_point, node_point]) for object_point, node_point in zip(objects.geometry, nearest_node_geoms)],\n",
    "    crs=G.crs,\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))\n",
    "local_roads.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#8FA1AB\", linewidth=1.15, alpha=0.78, label=\"roads\")\n",
    "local_nodes.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#7E8A92\", markersize=12, alpha=0.55, label=\"graph nodes\")\n",
    "nearest_lines.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#D84A3A\", linewidth=1.2, linestyle=\"--\", alpha=0.9, label=\"nearest-node link\")\n",
    "nearest_node_geoms.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#496A81\", markersize=70, edgecolor=\"white\", linewidth=0.8, label=\"nearest nodes\")\n",
    "objects.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#F2A541\", markersize=95, edgecolor=\"#222222\", linewidth=0.8, label=\"simulated objects\")\n",
    "set_plot_window(ax, objects, nearest_node_geoms, margin=30)\n",
    "ax.legend(loc=\"lower right\", frameon=True)\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-10",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Project objects with link edges\n",
    "\n",
    "`project_objects2urban_graph` splits nearest edges when needed and prepares changes that can be applied later. With `add_link_edge=True`, every object receives its own object node and a connector edge to the projected point on the graph.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-11",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.501010Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.501010Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.612562Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.612562Z"
    },
    "ExecuteTime": {
     "end_time": "2026-07-07T12:18:36.728071Z",
     "start_time": "2026-07-07T12:18:36.579167Z"
    }
   },
   "source": [
    "from iduedu import apply_urban_graph_changes, project_objects2urban_graph\n",
    "\n",
    "WALK_SPEED_M_PER_MIN = 5 * 1000 / 60\n",
    "\n",
    "changes_with_links, object2node_with_links = project_objects2urban_graph(\n",
    "    G,\n",
    "    objects,\n",
    "    WALK_SPEED_M_PER_MIN,\n",
    "    add_link_edge=True,\n",
    ")\n",
    "G_with_objects = apply_urban_graph_changes(G, changes_with_links)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(object2node_with_links.head())\n",
    "print(\"new nodes:\", 0 if changes_with_links.nodes_gdf is None else len(changes_with_links.nodes_gdf))\n",
    "print(\"new edges:\", 0 if changes_with_links.edges_gdf is None else len(changes_with_links.edges_gdf))\n",
    "print(\"deleted source edges:\", 0 if changes_with_links.edges_to_delete is None else len(changes_with_links.edges_to_delete))\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [

  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-13",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## Project objects without link edges\n",
    "\n",
    "With `add_link_edge=False`, objects are mapped to projection nodes on the graph. The graph still receives split edges and projection nodes, but there are no separate object-to-road connector edges.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-14",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.780089Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.780089Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.880834Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.880834Z"
    },
    "ExecuteTime": {
     "end_time": "2026-07-07T12:18:37.040713Z",
     "start_time": "2026-07-07T12:18:36.937609Z"
    }
   },
   "source": [
    "changes_without_links, object2node_without_links = project_objects2urban_graph(\n",
    "    G,\n",
    "    objects,\n",
    "    WALK_SPEED_M_PER_MIN,\n",
    "    add_link_edge=False,\n",
    ")\n",
    "G_projected = apply_urban_graph_changes(G, changes_without_links)\n",
    "\n",
    "print(object2node_without_links.head())\n",
    "print(\"new nodes:\", 0 if changes_without_links.nodes_gdf is None else len(changes_without_links.nodes_gdf))\n",
    "print(\"new edges:\", 0 if changes_without_links.edges_gdf is None else len(changes_without_links.edges_gdf))\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "object_index\n",
      "0    741\n",
      "1    742\n",
      "2    743\n",
      "3    744\n",
      "4    745\n",
      "Name: node_id, dtype: int64\n",
      "new nodes: 10\n",
      "new edges: 20\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "execution_count": 9
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-15",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.882678Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-07T12:10:55.882678Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-07T12:10:56.086109Z",

     "start_time": "2026-07-07T12:18:37.071468Z"
    }
   },
   "source": [
    "projection_nodes = G_projected.nodes_gdf.loc[object2node_without_links]\n",
    "projection_lines = gpd.GeoDataFrame(\n",
    "    geometry=[LineString([object_point, node_point]) for object_point, node_point in zip(objects.geometry, projection_nodes.geometry)],\n",
    "    crs=G.crs,\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 8))\n",
    "local_roads.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#8FA1AB\", linewidth=1.05, alpha=0.68, label=\"original roads\")\n",
    "if changes_without_links.edges_gdf is not None:\n",
    "    changes_without_links.edges_gdf.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#496A81\", linewidth=2.0, alpha=0.95, label=\"split graph edges\")\n",
    "projection_lines.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#D84A3A\", linewidth=1.2, linestyle=\"--\", alpha=0.9, label=\"object-to-projection mapping\")\n",
    "projection_nodes.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#496A81\", markersize=72, edgecolor=\"white\", linewidth=0.8, label=\"projection nodes\")\n",
    "objects.plot(ax=ax, color=\"#F2A541\", markersize=95, edgecolor=\"#222222\", linewidth=0.8, label=\"simulated objects\")\n",
    "set_plot_window(ax, objects, projection_nodes, margin=10)\n",
    "ax.legend(loc=\"lower right\", frameon=True)\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ],
   "outputs": [

   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-16",

   "source": [
    "## Method form\n",
    "\n",
    "For in-memory workflows, `UrbanGraph.project_objects` applies the changes and returns the updated graph plus the object-to-node mapping.\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "id": "objects-and-nearest-nodes-17",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-07-07T12:10:56.088499Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-07-07T12:10:56.087496Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-07-07T12:10:56.199530Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-07-07T12:10:56.199530Z"
    },
    "ExecuteTime": {
     "end_time": "2026-07-07T12:18:37.422148Z",
     "start_time": "2026-07-07T12:18:37.314356Z"
    }
   },
   "source": [
    "G_method, object2node_method = G.project_objects(\n",
    "    objects,\n",
    "    WALK_SPEED_M_PER_MIN,\n",
    "    max_dist=250,\n",
    "    add_link_edge=True,\n",
    ")\n",
    "\n",
    "G_method, object2node_method.head()\n"
   ],
